gene_region.label: AR transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical AR genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: AR encodes the androgen receptor, a ligand-regulated nuclear receptor transcription factor. This bundle emphasizes androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local AR bundle is androgen receptor signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: AR variants can alter or tag androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
condition_research_overview: Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: AR methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for AR.
Because AR biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use AR promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response.
Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000169083: AR coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: AR biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: AR: androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response
biorender_visuals.template_title: Intracellular Layout - Receptor Signaling from Plasma Membrane to Nucleus (2)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a AR androgen receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response and Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: AR CAG-repeat model is curated as a AR marker for Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context
research_context: Use this marker as a AR pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair AR CAG-repeat model with AR methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: AR CAG-repeat model or linked AR locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats AR CAG-repeat model as a gene-specific marker for Androgen signaling and prostate biology, Androgen insensitivity and endocrine review context and androgen receptor transcriptional control, steroid signaling, CAG-repeat modulation, prostate biology, and endocrine therapy response.

variant_records.clinical_parameter_summary: This record describes a recurrent model, haplotype, fusion, or loss-of-function class rather than a simple SNP row.
